examples.title: Cache Direct Mapping Simulation
examples.code:
FUNCTION getCacheLine(blockAddress, numCacheLines)
  RETURN blockAddress MOD numCacheLines

FUNCTION accessDirectMappedCache(address, cache, blockSize)
  blockNumber = address / blockSize
  cacheLine = getCacheLine(blockNumber, cache.numLines)
  tag = address / (blockSize * cache.numLines)
  
  IF cache.tag[cacheLine] == tag AND cache.valid[cacheLine] == TRUE
    RETURN cache.data[cacheLine]  // Cache hit
  ELSE
    // Cache miss - fetch from memory
    blockAddr = blockNumber * blockSize
    newBlock = readFromMemory(blockAddr, blockSize)
    cache.tag[cacheLine] = tag
    cache.valid[cacheLine] = TRUE
    cache.data[cacheLine] = newBlock
    RETURN newBlock[offset]  // Return requested data
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how direct-mapped cache determines which cache line a memory block should be placed in using modulo operation, and how cache hit/miss is determined by comparing tags.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for hit, O(blockSize) for miss

examples.title: LRU Cache Replacement Implementation
examples.description: Implements LRU replacement policy for cache simulation
examples.code:
class LRUCache:
    def __init__(self, capacity):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.cache = {}  # key: address, value: data
        self.access_order = []  # tracks LRU order
    
    def access(self, address):
        if address in self.cache:
            # Cache hit - move to end (most recently used)
            self.access_order.remove(address)
            self.access_order.append(address)
            return self.cache[address]
        else:
            # Cache miss
            if len(self.cache) >= self.capacity:
                # Evict LRU (first element)
                lru_address = self.access_order.pop(0)
                del self.cache[lru_address]
            
            # Load from memory (simulated)
            data = self.load_from_memory(address)
            self.cache[address] = data
            self.access_order.append(address)
            return data
    
    def load_from_memory(self, address):
        # Simulate memory access latency
        return f"data_at_{address}"

# Example usage
cache = LRUCache(capacity=4)
for addr in [100, 200, 100, 300, 400, 100]:
    print(f"Access {addr}: {cache.access(addr)}")
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates LRU cache replacement. When cache is full and a new block needs to be loaded, the least recently used block (first in access_order list) is evicted. On each hit, the accessed block is moved to the end to mark it as most recently used.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average with ordered dict, O(n) here

examples.title: Cache Hit Rate Calculation
examples.description: C program to calculate cache hit rate and average memory access time
examples.explanation: This C program simulates cache access tracking and calculates key performance metrics including hit rate, miss rate, and average memory access time (AMAT). It demonstrates the formula AMAT = Hit time + (Miss rate × Miss penalty).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of accesses

examples.title: Set-Associative Cache Implementation
examples.description: Java implementation of set-associative cache with FIFO replacement
examples.explanation: This Java implementation shows a 2-way set-associative cache with FIFO replacement policy. It demonstrates how to calculate set index and tag from a memory address, how to check for hits by comparing tags within a set, and how to handle misses by either adding a new block or replacing using FIFO.
